title: [lora_l2.c:602-616] thread join fallback to K_FOREVER after abort may hang on driver bug
description:
Completeness: Timeout handling category.

At lines 602-616, after k_thread_abort():


The K_FOREVER join assumes that after abort, the thread will immediately become joinable. This is normally true, but:

1. If the thread is stuck in a driver call that doesn't respond to abort (e.g., DMA wait), K_FOREVER could hang
2. Zephyr's abort is not guaranteed to interrupt all blocking operations

The comment at line 612 acknowledges 'typically immediate', but there's no timeout on the post-abort join.

Consider using a finite timeout even after abort (e.g., K_MSEC(100)), and if that fails, log a critical error and potentially k_panic() since the system is in an unrecoverable state.
close_reason: Fixed: changed K_FOREVER to K_MSEC(100) with error logging if join fails after abort
